bids.reasoning: At the opening (t=0.0), I am targeting my maximum-utility bid because there is no opponent history yet and no time pressure to concede. I therefore choose the top value on each issue, especially holding firm on issueC and issueD because they carry the highest importance in my preferences.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the very beginning of the negotiation, and the opponent’s only offer is far below my preferences. To avoid simply repeating my opening bid, I make a tiny concession on low-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issueC and other high-value terms.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04) and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer to me without conceding. I will avoid repeating my exact previous bid, but I will only concede on the relatively low-weight issueA while holding firm on the most important issues, especially issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82 by conceding only on the low-weight issueA while holding my most important issues, especially issueC and issueD, at top values. Since it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer without conceding, there is no reason to make major concessions yet, but changing issueA may test flexibility.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer for me without concession, so I should not give up high-weight issues. I am targeting a strong utility bid while making a small concession on the low-weight issueB and keeping issueC and issueD at my best values.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 because it is still very early, and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer without conceding. I have already conceded on lower-weight issues like issueA and issueB, while holding firm on my most important issues issueC and issueD since their behavior does not justify a larger concession yet.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same low-utility package. I will continue holding firm on my most important issues, especially issueC and issueD, while making small variation on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.14 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same low-value bid for me. I will hold firm on my most important issues, especially issueC and issueD, while making only a small variation on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my previous offer.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.86 because it is still early (t=0.16) and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering the same low-utility package for me. I will continue holding firm on my most important issues, issueC and issueD, while varying/conceding only on lower-weight issueA and issueB to avoid simply repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid by holding firm on my highest-weight issues, issueC and issueD, while conceding the lower-weight issueA and issueB toward the opponent’s repeated pattern. Since it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent has not conceded at all, I should not move on issueC or issueD yet, but this offer tests whether their insistence on valueA for the low-weight issues is enough to create movement.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while conceding mainly on the lower-weight issues and making only a small concession on issueD. The opponent has repeated the same offer with no concession and no messages, so I do not treat their position as strongly informative beyond preferring issueA/valueA and issueB/valueA; I continue to protect issueC, my highest-weight issue.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.725 for me, keeping the most important issueC near my top preference while continuing to concede on the lower-weight issueA and issueB. At t=0.22 it is still early, and the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer without conceding, so I should not make a large concession yet. Since they consistently propose valueA on issueA and issueB, I am leaving those as valueA while varying my previous bid to avoid repetition.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70, since it is only t=0.24 and the opponent has not conceded at all from their repeated low-utility offer. I have already conceded on lower-weight issueA and issueB, and this bid makes a modest additional tradeoff on issueD while keeping issueC near my top preference because it is by far my most important issue. There are no opponent messages, and the offer history suggests they are holding firm rather than revealing new flexibility.

I am targeting a relatively strong utility while conceding on the lower-weight issues issueA and issueB, since the opponent has repeatedly demanded valueA there. The opponent is holding completely firm on the same bid, and at t=0.26 there is still enough time to avoid major concessions on my most important issueC and important issueD, so I will keep issueC near my top preference and avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.705 by keeping the high-importance issueC at valueD while conceding on lower-weight issueA and issueB. Time is only at 0.28, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s repeated low-utility position, but I can make a modest concession on issueD to test for movement. The opponent has offered the exact same bid throughout and sent no messages, so their bidding shows firmness but no reciprocal concession yet.

I am targeting a moderate concession bid with utility around 0.57 for me, keeping issueC at valueD because issueC is by far my highest-weight issue. Since it is only t=0.30 and the opponent has not conceded at all, I should not drop issueC to their repeated valueB yet, but I can test a concession on issueD toward their repeated preference. There are no opponent messages, and their offers consistently signal interest in issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, issueC/valueB, and issueD/valueA.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility while holding firm on issueC, my most important issue, because the opponent has repeated the same low-utility offer without any concession. Since time is only 0.32, I will not yet concede issueC down to valueB; instead I will vary from my previous bid and keep concessions on the lower-weight issues A and B.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.586 by conceding on the lower-weight issues A, B, and D while holding firm on issueC, my most important issue. The opponent has repeatedly offered the exact same low-utility bid for me with no concession, so at t=0.34 I will not concede issueC to valueB yet, but I will make a bid closer to their apparent preferences on the other issues.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.57: I concede issueA, issueB, and issueD to the values the opponent has repeatedly offered, while preserving most of my value on the critical issueC by choosing valueD rather than their low-value valueB. At t=0.36 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their stagnant offer, and their history shows no concession or informative messages, so I will make a measured concession without dropping to their exact bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility while still protecting issueC, which is by far my most important issue; issueA, issueB, and issueD have already been conceded substantially. At t=0.38 there is still time before the deadline, and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering the same bid, so I should not yet drop issueC to their preferred valueB. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous bid by moving issueC back to my top value while keeping the other issues at values the opponent has consistently signaled.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around the mid-game, preserving value on issueC because it is by far my highest-weight issue while conceding the lower-weight issues A, B, and D toward the opponent's repeated pattern. The opponent has held completely firm on the same offer with no messages, so I should not collapse to their low-utility bid yet at t=0.40, but I can continue signaling flexibility on everything except issueC.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.51: conceding on issueA and the highly contested issueC while protecting issueD and issueB, which still matter to my payoff. At t=0.42 we are in mid-game, and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the same low-utility package to me; with no messages to qualify this, I am testing a tradeoff that may be more acceptable without dropping to their full demand.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.43: it concedes the opponent’s repeatedly demanded values on issueA, issueB, and especially issueC, while still holding issueD at my top value because issueD has meaningful weight for me. Since time is still mid-negotiation at t=0.44, I should not collapse fully to their exact standing offer yet, but their complete lack of concession suggests I need to test a stronger compromise than my last bid. There are no opponent messages, so I am relying only on the repeated offer pattern.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid by holding issueC at valueD, which is nearly as good for me as valueC, while conceding issueA, issueB, and issueD to the values the opponent has consistently proposed. The opponent has shown no concession at all and has repeatedly offered the same low-utility bid to me, so at t=0.46 I should not yet accept their full position but should test a package closer to their apparent priorities.

I am targeting a mid-level utility while preserving issueC, my most important issue, because the opponent has not conceded at all and keeps repeating the same low-utility bid for me. At t=0.48 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept their position; I will concede on issueA, issueB, and issueD, which are less valuable to me, while holding issueC at my top value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.41: still well above the repeated opponent offer, but with a meaningful concession on the most disputed issueC to valueB. The opponent has shown no concession at all and keeps repeating the same bid, so at t=0.50 I am making a mid-game compromise while preserving some value on issueD rather than accepting their full low-utility proposal.

At t=0.52 the opponent has held completely firm on the same bid, so I treat their stance as strong but not yet enough reason to accept their very low-utility offer. I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.57 by conceding on the lower-weight issues and issueD, while preserving issueC near my top value because it is by far my most important issue.

I am targeting a modest positive utility because the opponent has repeated the exact same offer throughout and has shown no concession at all. Although t=0.54 is only mid-game, the offer history strongly suggests they may be holding firm, so I am conceding on all issues to the bid they have consistently signaled while still scoring above reservation value.
